text:
There once was a wise old owl who lived in a cozy hollow. He had a keen ear for magic stories.

One moonlit night he heard of a enchanted book that could spin stars and weave dreams. He fluttered down to collect it. The townsfolk didn't believe such a thing. Why would they want such boring stories?

But when the owl showed them the book, even the birds flew away afraid.

The owl tucked away the book under his pillow. Night by night it spun stories into reality. Animals that had never dreamed before. Whispers that lulled nightmares into slumber. Even the darkest souls couldn't resist that sweet softness.

After a few dreamy nights the book spun into a deep slumber. The owl settled down with a book of his own dreams.

In his dreams he chased all sorts of strange things.

One dream was a haunted castle.

In the dream the owl hunted through eerie rooms

That weren't even really rooms -- just long stretches of endless night.
